body_txt: There are few better ways (short of No Party Like a Donner Party ) to demonstrate that a human character is desperate for food than to reduce him to eating rats. Although such animals are technically edible, their association with disease, garbage, and urban decay places them firmly on the "Unclean/Do Not Eat" list in most viewers' minds. And for right reason: they carry dangerous parasites, bacteria, viruses, infections and other things that make eating them risky or even lethal. This also applies to mice, cockroaches, dump-foraging seagulls, urban feral pigeons, mangy strays and other scrounging pests, although their small size makes some of these creatures unlikely candidates for Meal of Last Resort — although, again, the fact that people are going after such tiny morsels for food can also serve to highlight how desperate they've become. Commonly seen in After the End scenarios, in Failed States or during prolonged military sieges. The people of a Secret Sewer Society are also likely to have to subsist on the sewers' vermin if they don't fancy nightly robberies on surface stories every time they need groceries. Also Played for Laughs in cases where characters are simply too poor to afford even dog food . Eat That applies if the eating is done to win a bet or game show rather than survive. May be inverted when a Swarm of Rats or Rodents of Unusual Size are involved. Usually an alternative to Emergency Food Supply Animal , another way to showcase characters' famished need to eat whatever they can get... although if it's a pet rat that gets eaten, the two can overlap. It should be noted that rodents, insects, and other such animals are sometimes just part of a local food system and not eaten due to desperation; humans are omnivores and, ultimately, squeamishness is a learned trait and varies from culture to culture. Depictions of people eating such animals in this capacity fall under either Foreign Queasine , if the story treats foreign food as repulsive, or Weird World, Weird Food , if the idea is to use strange food to show that a foreign world or species is different from us. If a future society uses insect farming because livestock farming is not efficient enough for their needs, overlap may be possible with Future Food Is Artificial . Sister trope to I Ate WHAT?! , in which disgusting things that aren't even nominally food are eaten. Subtrope to both Poverty Food and Too Desperate to Be Picky .
